name: ◁ Harissa Honey Shrimp Skewers
servings: prep time: 15 minutes, plus 15 minutes marinating

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus more for greasing if needed
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus more for greasing if needed

ingredients.item: 1½ pounds (680 g) large shrimp (20 to 30 shrimp), peeled, deveined, and patted dry
ingredients.original: 1½ pounds (680 g) large shrimp (20 to 30 shrimp), peeled, deveined, and patted dry

ingredients.item: 3 small yellow onions, cut into quarters
ingredients.original: 3 small yellow onions, cut into quarters

ingredients.item:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley
Tunisian Harissa
ingredients.original:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley
Tunisian Harissa

ingredients.item: 1 If using bamboo skewers for this recipe, start by soaking them in water for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 If using bamboo skewers for this recipe, start by soaking them in water for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 In a large bowl, mix the harissa, honey, oil, and salt until well combined. Add the shrimp to the bowl and mix well, ensuring all the shrimp are coated. Let the shrimp marinate in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2 In a large bowl, mix the harissa, honey, oil, and salt until well combined. Add the shrimp to the bowl and mix well, ensuring all the shrimp are coated. Let the shrimp marinate in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3 Remove the shrimp from the fridge and skewer them, 4 or 5 shrimp per skewer, then place on a platter and let come to room temperature for 10 minutes. Reserve the leftover marinade. Toss the quartered onions in the bowl of leftover marinade to soak it up, then place them on the remaining skewers.
ingredients.original: 3 Remove the shrimp from the fridge and skewer them, 4 or 5 shrimp per skewer, then place on a platter and let come to room temperature for 10 minutes. Reserve the leftover marinade. Toss the quartered onions in the bowl of leftover marinade to soak it up, then place them on the remaining skewers.

ingredients.item: 4 Meanwhile, preheat an outdoor grill to 400°F (205°C). If grilling indoors, preheat a large cast-iron grill pan over medium heat. Grease the grill pan with oil to avoid the shrimp sticking.
ingredients.original: 4 Meanwhile, preheat an outdoor grill to 400°F (205°C). If grilling indoors, preheat a large cast-iron grill pan over medium heat. Grease the grill pan with oil to avoid the shrimp sticking.

ingredients.item: 5 If grilling outdoors, grill the skewers over direct, medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, turning the shrimp halfway through, until the shrimp curl and turn opaque. If grilling indoors, place the skewers on the grill pan and cook for 1 minute per side, or until the shrimp curl and turn opaque. Grill the onion skewers for 3 to 4 minutes per side, until charred and softened.
ingredients.original: 5 If grilling outdoors, grill the skewers over direct, medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, turning the shrimp halfway through, until the shrimp curl and turn opaque. If grilling indoors, place the skewers on the grill pan and cook for 1 minute per side, or until the shrimp curl and turn opaque. Grill the onion skewers for 3 to 4 minutes per side, until charred and softened.

ingredients.item: 6 Place the skewers on a platter along with the grilled onions and garnish with the chopped parsley. Serve more harissa in a bowl for dipping.
ingredients.original: 6 Place the skewers on a platter along with the grilled onions and garnish with the chopped parsley. Serve more harissa in a bowl for dipping.
